attractions.name: Alana's Food and Wine

attractions.description: Alana's Food and Wine, led by Chef Alana Shock, offers a unique culinary experience featuring seasonal, market-driven menus and expert wine pairings. Formerly a landmark restaurant in North Columbus, the business now focuses on intimate in-home dinner parties and a monthly private supper club. Alana's Food and Wine, 614Now

attractions.name: Alum Creek State Park
attractions.description: Alum Creek State Park is a popular 4,630-acre outdoor destination located just north of Columbus, Ohio, centered around a 3,387-acre reservoir. It features the largest inland beach in Ohio's state park system and offers a wide range of recreational activities including boating, camping, and hiking. Ohio Department of Natural Resources, Wikipedia
attractions.days_open: Open daily year-round. Ohio Traveler, Wikipedia
highlights: Largest inland beach in Ohio's state park system (3,000 feet)
3,387-acre reservoir for boating, jet skiing, and fishing
Extensive trail systems including 38 miles of bridle trails and 14 miles of mountain bike trails

Historic remnants of a settlement by freed slaves (Community of Africa) along the park border

attractions.name: Arena District
attractions.description: The Arena District is a premier 75-acre mixed-use development and neighborhood located in downtown Columbus, Ohio. It features a vibrant blend of professional sports venues, world-class entertainment, diverse dining options, and residential spaces, attracting over 6 million visitors annually. Arena District, Wikipedia

McFerson Commons (Arch Park): A 2.2-acre green space featuring the historic Union Station arch. Wikipedia
North Market: A historic public market with world-class food and independent merchants. North Market

attractions.name: Ash Cave (Hocking Hills State Park)
attractions.description: Ash Cave is the largest and most impressive recess cave in Ohio, featuring a massive horseshoe-shaped rim that spans 700 feet and rises 90 feet tall. Located in the southernmost reach of Hocking Hills State Park, it boasts a stunning seasonal waterfall that cascades into a sandy pool, all set within a lush gorge of hemlocks and hardwoods. The site is renowned for its scale, superb acoustics, and accessibility, making it a must-see natural wonder in the Hocking Hills region.
attractions.days_open: Open daily, 365 days a year including holidays.
highlights: Massive 700-foot wide horseshoe-shaped recess cave with 90-foot high rim.
Stunning 90-foot seasonal runoff waterfall at the cave's center.
Easy 1/4-mile handicap-accessible gorge trail to the cave floor.
Historic 'Pulpit Rock' used by early settlers for church services.
Excellent natural acoustics frequently used for concerts and events.
Lush forest environment featuring massive hemlocks and vibrant seasonal wildflowers.

attractions.name: Barcelona Restaurant and Bar
attractions.description: Tucked away in historic German Village, Barcelona Restaurant and Bar offers an authentic global dining experience inspired by seaside sangria and the Ramblas of Spain. The restaurant blends metropolitan chic with Old World European charm, set within a historic building that has served the community for over a century. Barcelona Columbus, Tripadvisor
attractions.days_open: Tuesday - Sunday

Consistently voted as having one of the best outdoor patios in Columbus.
Housed in a historic building from 1895 featuring a magnificent original oak bar from 1907.
Extensive, award-winning wine list and popular housemade sangria.
Regular live music events, including 'Midweek Music' showcases on Wednesdays.

attractions.name: Barley's Brewing Company
attractions.description: Barley's Brewing Company is the oldest operating brewpub in Central Ohio, serving award-winning craft ales and elevated pub fare since 1992. Located across from the Columbus Convention Center, it features a traditional English-style taproom, a second-floor vintage arcade called Brewcadia, and a unique event space in the basement known as Barley's Underground.
attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday
highlights: Central Ohio's oldest operating brewpub, established in 1992 Ohio.org
Home to 'Real Ale' and cask-conditioned beers served without added CO2 Points on Pints

attractions.name: Basi Italia
attractions.description: Nestled in the heart of the Victorian Village neighborhood, Basi Italia is a small casual feel-good restaurant featuring clean simple Italian fare with innovative twists. Owned by Chef Johnny Dornback and Trish Gentile, the eatery offers an approachable relaxed dining experience with a strong commitment to hospitality and seasonal, locally grown produce.
attractions.days_open: Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday

Vino on the Veranda - a weekly wine tasting event held every Wednesday.
Intimate and cozy indoor dining atmosphere in a historic residential setting.
Beautiful, lushly landscaped outdoor terrace and bar area.
Extensive and curated wine list featuring unique tasting descriptions.

attractions.name: Berlin Wall Segment at Capital University
attractions.description: A 2.8-ton, graffiti-covered segment of the historic Berlin Wall that once divided East and West Berlin. Originally brought to Columbus for the AmeriFlora '92 exposition, it now stands as a permanent monument to freedom and history on the Capital University campus. One side features colorful Western graffiti, while the other remains blank concrete with visible bullet holes from the East side.
attractions.address: Blackmore Library, Capital University, 1 College and Main St (approx. address for GPS: 661 College Ave or 2199 E Main St)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Public access to grounds/exterior), 24/7 (Exterior view)
highlights: Original 2.8-ton segment of the historic Berlin Wall.
Vibrant, authentic graffiti on the former West-facing side.
Pristine concrete with visible bullet holes on the former East-facing side.
Informative plaque detailing its journey from Berlin to Columbus via AmeriFlora '92.
Located in a peaceful, accessible courtyard setting on the Capital University campus.

attractions.name: Blacklick Woods Metro Park
attractions.description: Blacklick Woods Metro Park, opened in 1948 as the first in the Central Ohio system, encompasses 643 acres of diverse landscapes including beech-maple forests and seasonal swamp ponds. The park is renowned for its unique 40-foot-high Canopy Walk, which provides visitors with a bird's-eye view of the forest and includes rope bridges and a treehouse. It also features a designated National Natural Landmark preserve, two golf courses, and extensive trails for hiking and biking. Metro Parks, Wikipedia
attractions.days_open: Daily
highlights: Canopy Walk: A 40-foot high boardwalk through the treetops with rope bridges and a treehouse. Metro Parks
Walter A. Tucker State Nature Preserve: A 54-acre National Natural Landmark featuring old-growth beech-maple forest. Wikipedia
Golf Courses: Features both a 72-par Championship Course and a 9-hole Learning Course. Metro Parks
Nature Center: Offers educational exhibits and large wildlife observation windows. Experience Columbus
Blacklick Creek Greenway Trail: A 16-mile paved path connecting to other local parks. Metro Parks
Picnic Facilities: Multiple reservable lodges and free first-come, first-served picnic areas. Metro Parks

attractions.name: Blendon Woods Metro Park
attractions.description: Blendon Woods contains spectacular stream-cut ravines with exposed ripple rock sandstone and open fields surrounded by beech-maple and oak-hickory forests. The 653-acre park serves as a sanctuary for hundreds of birds and wildlife, featuring the 118-acre Walden Waterfowl Refuge and Thoreau Lake. Visitors can enjoy year-round activities including hiking, disc golf, and wildlife viewing from elevated observation shelters. (Metro Parks, Experience Columbus)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Metro Parks)

18-hole par-63 disc golf course (free to play)
Spectacular stream-cut ravines featuring ripple rock sandstone
Inclusive Playground specially designed for people of all abilities
Nature Center with wildlife viewing windows and discovery displays
10-acre Natural Play Area for unstructured outdoor play

attractions.name: BrewDog DogTap Columbus
attractions.description: BrewDog DogTap Columbus is the flagship U.S. brewery and taproom for the Scottish craft beer brand, offering an expansive "craft beer playground" in Canal Winchester (BrewDog). The venue features dozens of taps, a full-service kitchen, a dog park, and an onsite beer-themed hotel known as the DogHouse (Columbus on the Cheap). It serves as a major destination for beer enthusiasts, featuring regular events like trivia, live music, and interactive brewery tours (Experience Columbus).
attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday
highlights: Flagship U.S. brewery featuring dozens of BrewDog and guest craft beer taps.
Dog-friendly facility with an onsite dog park and extensive outdoor patio areas.
Home to the DogHouse Hotel, the world's first craft beer-themed hotel.
Interactive brewery tours and a "Beer Museum" exploring the history of craft brewing.
Full kitchen serving award-winning burgers, wings, and specialty tacos.

attractions.name: Brewery District (traditionally Old German Brewing District)
attractions.description: The Brewery District is a vibrant, historic neighborhood in Columbus, Ohio, that served as the heart of the city's beer manufacturing industry in the 19th century. Today, it has been revitalized into a premier entertainment and dining destination featuring restored industrial architecture, lively bars, and cultural landmarks like Shadowbox Live.

Shadowbox Live: The nation's largest resident theater company, known for sketch comedy and musical performances.
King Gambrinus Statue: A historic local landmark honoring the legendary 'King of Beer' in a pocket park on Front Street.
Historic Industrial Architecture: Well-preserved 19th-century German-style brick buildings, including the Born Brewery Building.
Dining and Nightlife: A dense collection of local favorites like Antiques on High, High Beck Tavern, and Local Cantina.

attractions.name: Butcher & Rose
attractions.description: Butcher & Rose is a modern and upscale steakhouse by Cameron Mitchell Restaurants that blends power and elegance with craft and creativity. Located in the Preston Centre in Downtown Columbus, it offers a paradoxical union of traditional steakhouse elements with a playful, modern flair and unparalleled service.

attractions.name: Cap City Fine Diner & Bar
attractions.description: Cap City Fine Diner & Bar is an upscale diner that offers a "new spin on old school" by serving American diner favorites and classic comfort food made modern. Part of the Cameron Mitchell Restaurants group, it is known for its retro-cool atmosphere, vibrant desserts, and signature boozy shakes. The restaurant features a large, semi-enclosed seasonal patio and is a favorite destination for both casual lunches and special occasions. (Cameron Mitchell Restaurants, Ohio.org)

attractions.name: Cedar Falls
attractions.description: Cedar Falls is the largest waterfall by volume in the Hocking Hills region, plunging 50 feet over sandstone cliffs into a picturesque gorge. The area is famous for its unique "Democracy Steps," a mathematically designed staircase that provides a rhythmic descent to the falls, and its historical significance as the former site of a 19th-century grist mill. (Ohio Department of Natural Resources, HockingHills.com)
attractions.days_open: Open daily, year-round (Hocking Hills State Park)
highlights: Largest waterfall by volume in the Hocking Hills region.
The "Democracy Steps," a mathematically designed staircase by architect Akio Hizume.
Scenic 50-foot waterfall cascading over Blackhand Sandstone.
Halfway point between Old Man's Cave and Ash Cave on the Buckeye Trail.
Historical site of a former 19th-century grist mill.
Unique rock formations and grottos surrounding Queer Creek.

attractions.name: Cedar Point Amusement Park
attractions.description: Cedar Point is a world-renowned 364-acre amusement park located on a scenic peninsula along the shores of Lake Erie. Known as the 'Roller Coaster Capital of the World,' it features a massive collection of 66 rides, including 18 world-class roller coasters, as well as a mile-long beach, live shows, and immersive dining experiences. Six Flags Cedar Point

Millennium Force - A legendary 310-foot tall giga coaster.
Top Thrill 2 - The world's tallest and fastest triple-launch strata coaster.
Cedar Point Shores - An 18-acre waterfront waterpark with slides and pools.
Hotel Breakers - A historic beachfront resort with direct park access.
HalloWeekends - A signature fall event featuring haunted houses and family-friendly activities.

attractions.name: Center of Science and Industry (COSI)
attractions.description: COSI, officially the Center of Science and Industry, is a nationally esteemed science center featuring more than 300 interactive exhibits. It is a dynamic, hands-on facility designed to engage, inspire, and transform lives through science, technology, and industry learning. COSI, Wikipedia

Opened in 2009, it serves as the home field for the Columbus Clippers, the Triple-A affiliate of the Cleveland Guardians. The ballpark is celebrated for its intimate fan experience, stunning skyline views, and has been honored multiple times as "Ballpark of the Year." Huntington Park Official Site, Experience Columbus

attractions.name: Columbus Museum of Art
attractions.description: Founded in 1878 as the Columbus Gallery of Fine Arts, the Columbus Museum of Art was the first art museum to register its charter with the state of Ohio. It houses a distinguished collection of American and European modern and contemporary art, folk art, glass art, and photography, providing transformative experiences with great art for everyone. Official Site, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Columbus Zoo and Aquarium
attractions.description: The Columbus Zoo and Aquarium is a world-renowned non-profit destination home to more than 7,000 animals representing over 800 species. Spanning over 400 acres, it offers immersive exhibits divided into eight global regions, including the expansive Heart of Africa and the historic North America Trek. Columbus Zoo and Aquarium, Wikipedia

attractions.description: Commonhouse Ales was a community-focused craft brewery and the first Certified B Corporation brewery in Ohio. Founded by craft beer pioneer Lenny Kolada, it aimed to blend high-quality brewing with social responsibility, supporting local nonprofits through its signature community fund.

attractions.description: Condado Tacos is a vibrant, art-filled restaurant specializing in build-your-own tacos, tequilas, and margaritas. attractions.name: Conkles Hollow State Nature Preserve
attractions.description: Conkles Hollow State Nature Preserve is a rugged, rocky gorge in Hocking County, considered one of the deepest in Ohio with sandstone cliffs rising nearly 200 feet. The preserve features a unique, cool microclimate that supports a lush wilderness of ferns, hemlocks, and spring wildflowers. Visitors can explore the area via the wheelchair-accessible Gorge Trail or the more strenuous Rim Trail, which offers panoramic views of the Hocking Hills region.

Multiple seasonal waterfalls cascading into the gorge.

attractions.name: Easton Town Center
attractions.category: Shopping & Entertainment Destination Easton Town Center
attractions.description: Easton Town Center is the Midwest’s premier shopping, dining, and entertainment destination, featuring over 200 best-in-class retailers and a variety of exciting dining options. attractions.name: Elevator Brewery & Draught Haus
attractions.description: Elevator Brewery & Draught Haus is a craft bar, micro-brewery, and chef-driven restaurant located in the historic Columbia Building in downtown Columbus. Originally built in 1897, the space offers an unmatched historical atmosphere featuring ornate woodwork and antique billiard tables. The establishment specializes in fine food and spirits, including a wide array of beers brewed on-site alongside house-made sodas and draft cocktails. Elevator Brewery

attractions.description: Endeavor Brewing and Spirits is Columbus’ first brewstillery, offering a unique combination of locally crafted beers and on-site distilled spirits. Inspired by the founders' global travels, the venue features a diverse taproom experience where visitors can enjoy internationally inspired styles and seasonal cocktails. The establishment is deeply rooted in the local community, serving as a hub for soccer fans and outdoor enthusiasts alike. (Endeavor Brewing and Spirits, Experience Columbus)

attractions.name: Ernest Warther Museum & Gardens
attractions.description: The Ernest Warther Museum & Gardens is a world-class facility built around the original residence and workshop of Ernest "Mooney" Warther, known as the World’s Master Carver. The museum showcases his collection of over sixty hand-sculpted, scaled, and mechanized steam engines carved from ebony, ivory, and walnut, alongside his wife Frieda's extensive collection of over 73,000 buttons.

attractions.name: Four String Brewing Company
attractions.description: Four String Brewing Company was a rock 'n' roll-themed craft brewery in Columbus, Ohio, founded in 2011 by Dan Cochran, a bassist and brewmaster. The brewery was known for its musical branding, including tap handles shaped like guitar necks and core beers like Brass Knuckle Pale Ale and Switchblade IPA. Although it expanded to a multi-million dollar production facility in the Hilltop neighborhood, the brewery permanently shuttered its doors in October 2018. Pats Pints, All About Beer, Drink Up Columbus

attractions.name: Franklin Park Conservatory and Botanical Gardens
attractions.description: A premier botanical landmark located two miles east of downtown Columbus, the Conservatory features exotic plant collections, seasonal exhibitions, and permanent Dale Chihuly glass artwork. Set within the 88-acre Franklin Park, it offers a blend of historic Victorian architecture and modern horticultural displays. Official Website, Tripadvisor

Hot Shop: Live glassblowing demonstrations where artists create unique glass works.

attractions.name: German Village
attractions.description: German Village is a historic neighborhood located just south of downtown Columbus, Ohio, originally settled in the mid-19th century by German immigrants. It is internationally recognized as one of the premier examples of urban neighborhood preservation, featuring meticulously restored red-brick homes and tree-lined, brick-paved streets. Today, it remains a vibrant community and a popular destination for its unique shops, local dining, and rich cultural heritage. German Village Society, Wikipedia
attractions.days_open: Daily (Visitors Center Sundays seasonally April-November) German Village Society
highlights: Schiller Park: A 23-acre community park featuring gardens, recreational facilities, and an amphitheater.

attractions.name: Ohio History Center
attractions.description: The Ohio History Center serves as the flagship museum and headquarters of the Ohio History Connection, chronicling the state's story from the Ice Age to the present. The Brutalist-style building houses extensive exhibits on archaeology, natural history, and mid-century life, as well as the State Archives of Ohio. (Ohio History Connection, Wikipedia)
attractions.days_open: Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
highlights: Conway Mastodon: A massive, reconstructed Ice Age skeleton that greets visitors at the natural history exhibit. (Ohio History Connection)
1950s Lustron House: A fully furnished, reconstructed steel home that offers an immersive look at mid-century American life. (Ohio History Connection)
Indigenous Wonders of Our World: An interactive exhibit exploring the Hopewell Ceremonial Earthworks, Ohio's first UNESCO World Heritage Sites. (Ohio History Connection)
Follow the Flag: A collection of conserved battle flags carried by Ohioans during the Civil War. (Ohio History Connection)
The Nature of Ohio: An exploration of the state's rich natural history through themes of geology, plants, and animals. (Ohio History Connection)
Village Visions: A preview exhibit of the upcoming reimagined Ohio Village. (Ohio History Connection)

attractions.name: Ohio Stadium (Ohio State Athletics)
attractions.description: Ohio Stadium, also known as "The Horseshoe" or "The Shoe," is one of the most recognizable and iconic landmarks in college athletics. Located on the banks of the Olentangy River, it serves as the home field for the Ohio State Buckeyes and is one of the largest on-campus football stadiums in the United States (Ohio State Athletics, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 411 Woody Hayes Dr, Columbus, OH 43210 (Ohio State Athletics)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (standard tours), occasional weekends (Ohio State Athletics)
highlights: Iconic horseshoe-shaped design by architect Howard Dwight Smith (Wikipedia)
Historic Rotunda featuring stained glass murals of Buckeye football squads (Wikipedia)
Home to the Ohio State Buckeyes football team since 1922 (Ohio State Athletics)
Hosting major concerts and events, including international soccer matches (Wikipedia)
Listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 1974 (Wikipedia)
Capacity of over 102,000 spectators (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Ohio Statehouse
attractions.description: The Ohio Statehouse is the state capitol building and seat of government for the U.S. state of Ohio, located on Capitol Square in downtown Columbus. Built between 1839 and 1861, it is a premier example of Greek Revival architecture and houses the Ohio General Assembly and the ceremonial offices of the Governor. (Wikipedia, Ohio.org)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed on state holidays) (Ohio Statehouse)
highlights: Greek Revival architecture constructed with local Columbus limestone (Ohio Statehouse)
Interactive Museum Education Center located on the ground floor (Ohio Statehouse)
A grand Rotunda featuring a floor made of nearly 5,000 pieces of hand-cut marble (Wikipedia)
The "Ohioans in Space" painting honoring famous astronauts like Neil Armstrong and John Glenn (Columbus Dispatch)
A 10-acre Capitol Square park filled with historic monuments, including the William McKinley Monument (Ohio Statehouse)
A magnificent glass-enclosed Atrium connecting the Statehouse and Senate Building (Ohio Statehouse)

attractions.name: Ohio Theatre
attractions.description: The Ohio Theatre is a historic 2,791-seat performing arts center and former movie palace located on Capitol Square in Downtown Columbus. Known as the 'Official Theatre of the State of Ohio,' it features opulent Spanish-Baroque architecture and hosts over 100 performances annually, including Broadway shows, concerts, and classical music Columbus Association for the Performing Arts, Wikipedia)
attractions.days_open: Open daily for performances; Ticket Center open Monday–Friday; Sunday (performance days only) Columbus Association for the Performing Arts, Ticketmaster
highlights: Designated a National Historic Landmark in 1977 Columbus Association for the Performing Arts
Opulent Spanish-Baroque architecture with a 21-foot-high chandelier Columbus Association for the Performing Arts
Home to the Columbus Symphony, BalletMet, and Broadway in Columbus Columbus Association for the Performing Arts
Hosts the popular CAPA Summer Movie Series Columbus Association for the Performing Arts
Saved from demolition in 1969 and completely restored Wikipedia)
Original movie palace features including a grand lobby and intricate gold leaf Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Ohio Union
attractions.description: The Ohio Union serves as the central student activity center for The Ohio State University, providing a hub for dining, recreation, and campus events. Established in 1910 as the first student union at a public university, the current facility opened in 2010 and houses numerous student services and meeting spaces. (Wikipedia, Ohio Union)
attractions.days_open: Daily (subject to university and federal holidays) (Ohio Union Hours)
highlights: First student union built by an American public university (Wikipedia)
Home to Sloopy's Diner, a classic university-themed restaurant (Ohio Union)
Houses the Keith B. Key Center for Student Leadership and Service (Ohio Union)
Features 34 dynamic meeting and event spaces (Ohio Union)
Headquarters for the D-Tix Discount Ticket Program (Ohio Union)
Includes an on-site Barnes & Noble bookstore and US Bank branch (Ohio Union)

attractions.name: Old Man's Cave at Hocking Hills State Park
attractions.description: Old Man's Cave is a massive recess cave and landmark within Hocking Hills State Park, featuring a deep gorge carved by wind and water into Blackhand sandstone. The area is home to stunning waterfalls, high cliffs, and unique rock formations, serving as the central hub and most popular destination in the park ohiodnr.gov, minivanadventures.com.
attractions.days_open: Open 365 days a year thehockinghills.org
highlights: Upper Falls, a picturesque waterfall cascading into a clear pool hockinghills.com
Devil's Bathtub, a unique swirling basin carved into the sandstone hockinghills.com
The Sphinx Head, a natural rock formation resembling a profile thehockinghills.org
Grandma Gatewood Trail, a 6-mile path connecting Old Man's Cave to Cedar Falls and Ash Cave hockinghills.com
The massive recess cave itself, once the home of 19th-century hermit Richard Rowe chaletshh.com
Hocking Hills Visitor Center, featuring interactive displays and raptor enclosures ohiodnr.gov

attractions.name: Olentangy Caverns (historically Olentangy Indian Caverns)
attractions.description: Olentangy Caverns is a historic family-friendly destination featuring a network of caves formed millions of years ago by an underground river. Once used by the Wyandot Indians as a refuge, the site now offers self-guided tours across three subterranean levels alongside a wide range of outdoor activities like gem mining and a petting zoo. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 1779 Home Road, Delaware, OH 43015 Official Website
attractions.days_open: Daily during the season (March 19 - November 1) Official Website
highlights: Self-guided tours through three levels of limestone caverns (55, 75, and 105 feet underground) Official Website
50,000-square-foot animal encounter area featuring Highland cows and deer Experience Columbus
Treasure Hunt Maze with hidden vending machines for prizes Destination Delaware
Gem mining station where visitors can pan for gemstones and arrowheads Official Website
Bigfoot Walking Trail, a one-mile nature hike with local wildlife and wooden statues Experience Columbus
Seasonal special events such as Cave Yoga and "Made by Me" kids\

attractions.name: Olentangy Trail (Olentangy Greenway Trail)
attractions.description: The Olentangy Trail is a popular 14-mile multi-use greenway that winds through the heart of Columbus, following the scenic Olentangy River. It connects neighborhoods, several major city parks, and the Ohio State University campus, offering a seamless route for cyclists, runners, and nature enthusiasts from Worthington to downtown Columbus (Columbus Recreation and Parks, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 3901 N High St, Columbus, OH 43214 (Whetstone Park Trailhead) / 7770 Olentangy River Rd, Columbus, OH 43235 (Worthington Hills Park Trailhead)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday (7 days a week) (Waze)
highlights: Connects several major parks including Whetstone, Antrim, and Northmoor Parks (Columbus Recreation and Parks)
Runs directly through the heart of The Ohio State University campus and by Ohio Stadium (TrailLink)
Offers scenic river views with multiple bridge crossings and wooded sections (FLOW)
Links to the Scioto Greenway Trail for expanded travel into downtown and German Village (National Recreation Trails)
Features the Columbus Park of Roses, a 13-acre park with over 12,000 roses (Wikipedia)
Part of the Great American Rail Trail and the Ohio to Erie Trail (TrailLink)

attractions.name: Palace Theatre
attractions.description: Designed in the spirit of France’s Palais de Versailles, the historic 2,695-seat Palace Theatre is a premier performing arts venue located at the base of the LeVeque Tower. Originally opened as a vaudeville house in 1926, it features an elegant interior with chandeliers, a grand staircase, and exceptional acoustics CAPA, IBDB.
attractions.days_open: Daily for scheduled performances; Box Office open on performance days only CAPA, Ticketmaster
highlights: Versailles-inspired architecture with crystal chandeliers and a grand staircase CAPA
Exceptional acoustics originally designed for unamplified vaudeville performances IBDB
Home to the Broadway in Columbus series and a wide variety of concerts and live events CAPA
Unique 'Hall of Autographed Pictures' featuring decades of legendary performers CAPA
Historic location integrated into the base of the iconic LeVeque Tower Wikipedia

attractions.name: Pins Mechanical Company (Downtown Columbus)
attractions.description: Pins Mechanical Company is a lively social destination that blends a high-energy bar scene with nostalgic, old-school entertainment. The venue features duckpin bowling, dozens of classic pinball machines, and a variety of other games alongside a diverse menu of craft beers and creative cocktails. Yelp, Rise Brands
attractions.days_open: Daily (Monday-Sunday) Columbus, OH | Downtown | Pins Mechanical Co.

Dozens of classic and modern pinball machines
Extensive craft beer list and playful specialty cocktails
Dog-friendly environment both inside and on the patios

attractions.name: Polaris Fashion Place
attractions.description: Polaris Fashion Place is Central Ohio’s premier shopping destination, featuring a two-level indoor mall and an outdoor promenade. It offers a mix of over 170 national, regional, and local retail, dining, and entertainment options, including market-exclusive brands. Polaris Fashion Place, Wikipedia

Ample parking with valet services and limo/bus accommodations Polaris Fashion Place

attractions.name: Puttshack Columbus
attractions.description: Puttshack is a tech-infused social entertainment destination that reimagines the classic game of mini golf with a high-energy atmosphere and competitive socializing. The Columbus location features two tech-driven nine-hole courses and three exclusive "Challenge Holes," which are private suites equipped with rotating mini golf games and dedicated service. Puttshack, PR Newswire
attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday
highlights: Tech-infused "Trackaball" scoring system for competitive mini golf

attractions.name: Rock House at Hocking Hills State Park
attractions.description: Rock House is a unique tunnel-like cave situated midway up a 150-foot cliff of Black Hand sandstone. As the only true cave in Hocking Hills State Park, it features a 200-foot-long corridor with seven natural Gothic-arched windows and impressive sandstone columns. (HockingHills.com, ODNR)
attractions.days_open: Daily (ODNR)
highlights: Only true cave in Hocking Hills State Park (HockingHills.com)
200-foot-long tunnel corridor midway up a 150-foot cliff (ODNR)

attractions.name: Schiller Park
attractions.description: Schiller Park is a historic 23.45-acre municipal park located in the heart of the German Village neighborhood. Originally established in 1867, the park serves as a community hub featuring scenic gardens, recreational facilities, and a popular outdoor amphitheater for live performances. (Wikipedia), Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Official Website)
highlights: Huntington Gardens, featuring meticulously maintained floral displays and volunteer-led gardening. (Wikipedia))
Free Shakespeare in the Park performances held during summer months at the outdoor amphitheater. (Experience Columbus)
A scenic fishing pond equipped with a historic bridge and the "Umbrella Girl" statue. (Official Website)
Schiller Community Center, which offers various indoor recreational programs and facilities. (German Village Society)
Athletic amenities including lighted tennis courts, basketball courts, and pickleball overlays. (Official Website)
The historic Friedrich von Schiller statue, a landmark dedicated by the German-born residents in 1891. (Wikipedia))

attractions.name: Scioto Audubon Metro Park
attractions.description: Scioto Audubon Metro Park is a 120-acre urban oasis transformed from a former industrial brownfield into a thriving bird sanctuary and recreational destination. Located on the Whittier Peninsula along the banks of the Scioto River, it offers a unique blend of restored wetlands and prairies set against the backdrop of the downtown Columbus skyline. Metro Parks, Wikipedia
attractions.days_open: Monday-Sunday Metro Parks
highlights: The 35-foot tall, 7,000-square-foot outdoor rock climbing wall, one of the largest free outdoor climbing structures in the U.S. Metro Parks
The 18,000-square-foot Grange Insurance Audubon Center, providing educational programs and bird watching opportunities. Audubon
Extensive trail systems including the Scioto Greenway Trail and the Hermit Thrush Trail. Metro Parks

attractions.name: ScottsMiracle-Gro Field (formerly Lower.com Field)
attractions.description: ScottsMiracle-Gro Field is a state-of-the-art, soccer-specific stadium located in the Astor Park development of downtown Columbus. Opened in 2021 as Lower.com Field, the $314 million venue serves as the home for the Columbus Crew (MLS) and features a massive 220,000 square-foot canopy that covers all 20,371 seats. Wikipedia, Columbus Crew Stadium
attractions.days_open: Tuesday – Saturday (Crew Shop), plus match and event days. Columbus Soccer Shop

attractions.name: Seventh Son Brewing Co.
attractions.description: Seventh Son Brewing Co. has been producing exceptional beers at the intersection of 4th & 4th in Columbus’ historic Italian Village since 2013 Seventh Son Brewing Co.. Consistently voted Columbus' best brewery, it features a comfortable taproom, an expansive upstairs patio, and a rotating schedule of food trucks Ohio.org.

attractions.name: Shadowbox Live
attractions.description: Shadowbox Live is the nation's largest resident theatre ensemble, featuring a troupe of professionally trained "metaperformers" who write, produce, and perform original works. The venue specializes in a unique blend of sketch comedy, rock-n-roll music, and dance, offering an immersive cabaret-style experience in the heart of Columbus. Shadowbox Live Official Website, Tripadvisor

highlights: Nation's largest resident theater ensemble Shadowbox Live

attractions.name: Short North Arts District
attractions.description: The Short North Arts District is a vibrant and inclusive destination of discovery and a community for creators in the heart of Columbus, Ohio. Known as the 'Art and Soul' of the city, this historic neighborhood is home to over 300 independent businesses, including dozens of galleries, unique boutiques, and award-winning restaurants. Short North About Page, Experience Columbus
attractions.days_open: Daily (Businesses typically open daily; Administrative office open Mon-Fri) Short North Official Site

Lighted Arches: 17 iconic, historic-style metal arches that illuminate High Street at night. Wikipedia

Dining & Nightlife: A high concentration of some of the city's best restaurants, rooftop bars, and entertainment venues. Short North Eat & Drink

attractions.name: Skully's Music-Diner
attractions.description: Skully's Music-Diner is a legendary staple in Columbus, Ohio, known for its electric atmosphere and diverse live entertainment. Originally established in 1994 near the Ohio State campus, it has grown into a premier venue in the Short North Arts District, hosting everything from local DJ nights to major touring acts. The venue is particularly famous for its long-running "Ladies 80s" dance party and has been repeatedly voted the city's best dance club. Skully's Music-Diner

attractions.name: Slate Run Metro Park and Living Historical Farm
attractions.category: Park, Nature Preserve, and Living History Museum
attractions.description: Slate Run Metro Park is a 1,705-acre nature preserve featuring diverse habitats like grasslands, wetlands, and forests. Its centerpiece is the Slate Run Living Historical Farm, where visitors can experience 1880s Ohio farm life through authentic buildings, livestock, and costumed interpreters (Metro Parks, Wikipedia).

attractions.name: Smokehouse Brewing Company (Smokehouse Brewing Company)
attractions.description: Smokehouse Brewing Company was a premier award-winning microbrewery and barbecue restaurant in Columbus, Ohio, that operated for over 25 years (Smokehouse Brewing Company). It was renowned for its slowly smoked meats prepared over hickory logs and its unique, small-batch ales brewed on-site (Smokehouse Brewing Company PDF). Although it closed its doors in early 2024, it remains a celebrated part of the city's craft beer and culinary history (Reddit).
attractions.days_open: Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Smokehouse Brewing Company)

attractions.name: Southern Theatre
attractions.description: The Southern Theatre is the oldest surviving theatre in central Ohio, originally opened in 1896 as part of a performance space and hotel complex. After an extensive restoration in the late 1990s, it reopened as an intimate 925-seat venue renowned for its architectural beauty and superb acoustics. Today, it serves as a premier destination for concerts, opera, jazz, and community events in downtown Columbus. CAPA Official Site, Cinema Treasures

attractions.name: The Actual Brewing Company
attractions.description: The Actual Brewing Company was a craft brewery in Columbus known for its philosophy of "brewing beer at the intersection of science, art and religion." The company produced innovative, small-batch beers using an in-house canning line and operated a cozy, "nerdy" taproom filled with vintage arcade games and consoles. (Pat's Pints, Breakfast With Nick)
attractions.days_open: Tuesday–Sunday (Historical) (Only In Your State)

attractions.name: The Book Loft of German Village
attractions.description: The Book Loft of German Village is one of the nation's largest independent bookstores, featuring 32 rooms of bargain books and unique merchandise. Housed in pre-Civil War era buildings that once served as general stores, a saloon, and a nickelodeon cinema, it offers a maze-like 'literary labyrinth' experience for visitors. The store stocks nearly 750,000 volumes, including a vast collection of children's books and discounted remainders.

attractions.name: The Burgundy Room at The Makoy Center
attractions.description: The Burgundy Room is a sophisticated event space located within The Makoy Center, featuring an elegant French Art Deco ambiance. Spanning 7,000 square feet, it is designed for medium-sized gatherings like weddings and corporate events, complete with a built-in bar, a permanent stage, and a spacious dance floor. The Makoy Center, Zola

attractions.name: The Guild House
attractions.description: The Guild House is an upscale artisan eatery featuring creative American cuisine with a strong emphasis on locally grown ingredients and seasonal offerings. Located in the heart of the Short North Arts District, it provides an airy and comfortable atmosphere combined with genuine hospitality. The restaurant is part of the Cameron Mitchell Restaurants collection and showcases a "culinary guild" of artisans dedicated to high standards (The Guild House, Experience Columbus).

attractions.name: The Ohio State University (Columbus Campus)
attractions.description: The Ohio State University is a leading public research university and the flagship institution of Ohio's state university system. Spanning over 1,700 acres in the heart of Columbus, the campus is renowned for its historic landmarks, vibrant student culture, and world-class athletic programs centered at Ohio Stadium. It offers a blend of historic architecture and modern facilities, serving as a hub for innovation and education.
attractions.days_open: Open Daily
highlights: The Oval: The historic and social center of campus life.
Ohio Stadium ("The Shoe"): Iconic home of the Buckeye football team and one of the largest stadiums in the world.

Wexner Center for the Arts: A world-renowned contemporary arts center and laboratory for new art forms.
Mirror Lake: A scenic and historic gathering spot on campus.
Chadwick Arboretum: Over 60 acres of diverse gardens and plant collections.

attractions.name: The Olde Towne Tavern
attractions.description: The Olde Towne Tavern is a friendly neighborhood restaurant and bar located in the heart of the historic Olde Towne East neighborhood. Housed in a former apothecary shop, it offers a cozy atmosphere with a rustic-chic interior, a spacious outdoor brick patio, and a diverse menu of traditional bar food with healthy and creative twists. (Official Website, Experience Columbus)

attractions.name: The Oval (The Ohio State University) (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: The Oval is an 11-acre quadrangle that serves as the historic and geographic center of The Ohio State University's Columbus campus. Often referred to as the campus "living room," it is a large field of grass, trees, and paths that serves as a central gathering spot for social interaction, relaxation, and informal activities. (Wikipedia, Ohio State University)

attractions.name: The Pearl Restaurant, Tavern & Oyster Bar
attractions.description: The Pearl is a well-polished culinary jewel and casual gastropub serving seasonal seafood, unique shareables, and handmade tavern fare. Located in the heart of the Short North Arts District, it features an oyster bar and a warm, lively atmosphere with a focus on made-from-scratch food and classically inspired cocktails.

attractions.name: The Refectory Restaurant & Wine Shop
attractions.description: The Refectory is a premier fine dining destination in Columbus, Ohio, housed in a historic 19th-century church building. Under the direction of Executive Chef Richard Blondin, the restaurant offers an exceptional blend of contemporary American cuisine and classic French techniques, complemented by a world-class, award-winning wine cellar. Refectory Our Story

attractions.name: The Scioto Mile
attractions.description: The Scioto Mile is a vibrant urban oasis in downtown Columbus, comprising over 145 acres of lush parkland along the riverfront. It features an integrated system of parks, multi-use trails, and interactive fountains that connect the Arena District to the Whittier Peninsula.

attractions.name: Topiary Garden at Old Deaf School Park
attractions.description: Topiary Park, officially the Topiary Garden at Old Deaf School Park, is a 9.2-acre urban garden in downtown Columbus's Discovery District. It is the only park in the world designed as a living recreation of a painting, specifically Georges Seurat’s 1884 post-impressionist masterpiece, "A Sunday Afternoon on the Island of La Grande Jatte." The garden features 54 life-sized topiary figures sculpted from yew trees, arranged to replicate the people, animals, and boats depicted in the original artwork (Wikipedia, City of Columbus).
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Columbus Recreation and Parks)

attractions.name: Village of Sugarcreek
attractions.description: Known as "The Little Switzerland of Ohio," Sugarcreek is a picturesque village famous for its Alpine-style architecture, Swiss heritage, and role as the "Gateway to Amish Country." The town is home to the World's Largest Cuckoo Clock and celebrates its history through cheese-making traditions and the annual Ohio Swiss Festival.
attractions.address: 410 South Broadway (Village Hall), 106 West Main Street (Alpine Hills Museum)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Saturday (Most attractions and businesses are closed on Sundays)
highlights: World's Largest Cuckoo Clock
Alpine Hills Historical Museum
Brick Wall Sculptures
Age of Steam Roundhouse Museum
The Farm at Walnut Creek (Petting Zoo & Farm Tour)
Ohio Swiss Festival (Annual Celebration)

attractions.name: Wexner Center for the Arts
attractions.description: Inaugurated in 1989 as a multidisciplinary laboratory for contemporary art, the Wexner Center is a dynamic cultural institution based on The Ohio State University’s main campus. Housed in a signature work of late 20th-century architecture by Peter Eisenman, it champions artistic exploration across visual arts, performance, film, and video (Wexner Center History & Architecture).
attractions.days_open: Tuesday–Sunday (Galleries); Monday–Friday (Café); Daily (Store and Visitor Desk) (Wexner Center Visit Page)
highlights: Architecture by Peter Eisenman, featuring a landmark design that references both history and the future (Wexner Center Architecture)
A world-class Film/Video program featuring curated screenings, restoration festivals, and new restorations (Wexner Center Film/Video)
The Box, a dedicated space for viewing provocative and experimental video works throughout the year (Wexner Center The Box)
Artist Residency Awards that support the research and production of pioneering contemporary art (Wexner Center Residencies)
Heirloom Café, offering unique breakfast and lunch options using locally sourced ingredients (Wexner Center Café)
The Wexner Center Store, featuring an extensive collection of art books, unique jewelry, and housewares (Wexner Center Store)

attractions.name: Wolf's Ridge Brewing Company
attractions.description: Established in 2013, Wolf’s Ridge is a family-owned brewery and restaurant known for its upscale American cuisine and inventive craft brews. It features three distinct spaces—a Dining Room, a Taproom, and the Hickory Room—dedicated to providing unique dining experiences and premium beer to the Columbus community. (Wolf's Ridge Brewing, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Yoder's Amish Home
attractions.category: Museum / Working Farm / Educational Farm Ohio's Amish Country
attractions.description: Yoder’s Amish Home is a 116-acre authentic working farm that provides visitors an immersive look into Amish history and culture. The property features guided tours of traditional homes, a one-room schoolhouse, and a historic barn built in 1885. Guests can experience the simple lifestyle through scenic buggy rides, interacting with farm animals, and visiting the on-site bakery and gift shop. Ohio's Amish Country
attractions.address: 6050 State Route 515, Millersburg, OH 44654 Yoder's Amish Home
attractions.days_open: Thursday, Friday, Saturday Yoder's Amish Home
highlights: Guided tours of two authentic Amish homes furnished in traditional style
Visit to a historic 140-year-old barn built in 1885
Interactive experience at a genuine one-room Amish schoolhouse
Scenic buggy rides through the rolling countryside of Holmes County
Opportunity to pet and feed various farm animals in the barnyard area
On-site bakery and gift shop featuring handmade Amish crafts and treats

attractions.name: Zaftig Brewing Company Official Website
attractions.description: Established in 2013, Zaftig Brewing Company is Worthington's only craft brewery, well-known in the Columbus area for its high-gravity, full-bodied ales. The brewery pushes the boundaries of craft beer, offering a welcoming taproom with a rotating selection of beers, wine, and liquor. It is a family-friendly and dog-friendly destination that frequently hosts food trucks and live community events. Official Website, Ohio.org
